other_observers: Kathy Mihm Dunning and her husband; Found on 11/12/2011 by Steve Mlodinow and Tim Smart
specific_location: On an island (or at times an island) in the South Plate River on the main road, U.S. Hwy 385 north from I-76 to Julesburg.
description: A small dove that walked on the ground. Head, nape, and breast scaly in appearance. Bill two-tone with dull redish base and dull blackish distal two thirds. Eye dark brown. Face tan with ruddy tones. Crown darker than hindneck and scaly. Back, sandy brown but with olive overtones and not markings on it. Tail not tapered and with small white tips (as seen in a Joe Roller photo). Darkish comma markings on wings. Wings have faint rusty wash. Primaries not visible. Belly with a sandy wash. Breast with faint ruddy hues.Behaviors: Walked only. It was either feeding on small grass seeds or picking up grit from the road. Others saw it fly.Call: none heardPlumage: adult
photographer: Photographer: bill maynard, bill maynard, bill maynard
